'Wabi' refers to 'imperfect' or 'imperfect' – like the beauty of things that are slightly broken or worn. And 'sabi' is about 'imperfect' or 'imperfect' – the appreciation of things that are slightly worn or imperfect. So together, 'wabi-sabi' is the appreciation of beauty in imperfection – the idea that things that are not perfect are more beautiful.
